aiExplain.tooltip: Ask AI to explain this setting
aiExplain.title: About: {{topic}}
aiExplain.notReady: AI assistant is not configured. Open Settings → API to set it up.
aiExplain.disclaimer: AI responses can be incorrect — verify important changes.

core.configSavedRestart: Configuration saved! Please restart B4 for core settings to take effect.
core.coreRestartWarning: Core settings require B4 restart to take effect
core.discardConfirm: Are you sure you want to discard all unsaved changes? This action cannot be undone.

tabs.payloadsDesc: Capture real payloads from live traffic

Control.title: Core Controls
Control.description: Control core service and config operations
Control.resetConfirm: This will reset configuration to defaults. Your sets, web server settings, and geo file paths are preserved. Everything else (queue, logging, discovery, API tokens, devices, interfaces, timezone, SOCKS5, MTProto, MSS clamping) will be wiped.

Queue.title: Queue & Packet Processing
Queue.description: Configure netfilter queue and packet processing parameters
Queue.packetMarkHelp: Netfilter packet mark for iptables rules (default: 32768)
Queue.workerThreadsHelp: Number of worker threads for processing packets simultaneously (default 4)
Queue.tcpLimitHelp: Max TCP packets per connection to inspect (default 19). Sets cannot exceed this value.
Queue.udpLimitHelp: Max UDP packets per connection to inspect (default 8). Sets cannot exceed this value.

Logging.title: Service
Logging.description: Service controls, logging, timezone, and process limits
Logging.logLevelHelp: Set the verbosity of logging output
Logging.errorFilePathHelp: Full path to error log file
Logging.memoryLimitHelp: Soft cap on b4 memory use. Accepts e.g. 128MiB, 256m, 1g. Leave empty or 'auto' for half of system RAM. Use 'off' to disable.

Feature.title: Feature Flags
Feature.firewallMonitorHelp: Interval for monitoring B4 iptables/nftables rules
Feature.firewallMonitorWarning: Warning: This disables automatic monitoring of B4 iptables/nftables
Feature.natMasqueradeDesc: Enable NAT masquerade for container/gateway setups
Feature.masqueradeInterfaceDesc: Select output interface for masquerade (empty = all interfaces)
Feature.masqueradeAllInterfaces: Masquerade will apply to all output interfaces if none is selected.
Feature.networkInterfacesDesc: Select interfaces to monitor (empty = all interfaces)
Feature.listenAllInterfaces: B4 will listen on all available interfaces if none are selected.

WebServer.title: Web Server
WebServer.description: Configure the web UI server binding and TLS
WebServer.bindAddressHelp: IP to bind (0.0.0.0 = all, 127.0.0.1 = localhost only, :: = all IPv6)
WebServer.tlsCertHelp: Path to TLS certificate file (empty = HTTP mode)
WebServer.tlsKeyHelp: Path to TLS private key file (empty = HTTP mode)
WebServer.usernameHelp: Leave both empty to disable authentication
WebServer.passwordHelp: Password for logging into the web UI
WebServer.authPartialWarning: Authentication is only enabled when both username and password are set. Partial credentials will be ignored.
WebServer.authHttpWarning: Authentication credentials will be sent over unencrypted HTTP. Configure TLS certificates above for secure HTTPS transport.

Socks5.title: SOCKS5 Proxy
Socks5.description: Built-in SOCKS5 proxy that routes traffic through DPI bypass engine
Socks5.enableDesc: Built-in SOCKS5 proxy that routes traffic through DPI bypass engine
Socks5.bindAddressHelp: IP to bind (0.0.0.0 = all, 127.0.0.1 = localhost only)
Socks5.restartNote: Restart B4 after changing SOCKS5 settings for changes to take effect.

MTProto.title: MTProto Proxy
MTProto.description: Built-in Telegram MTProto proxy with fake-TLS obfuscation
MTProto.serverDesc: A Telegram proxy that clients connect to with a secret (on this LAN or remotely, e.g. from a cellular network). Requires the toggle below. Not needed for the 'Telegram over WebSocket' routing mode.
MTProto.enableDesc: Telegram proxy that wraps traffic in fake TLS to bypass DPI
MTProto.bindAddressHelp: IP to bind (0.0.0.0 = all, 127.0.0.1 = localhost only)
MTProto.fakeSNIHelp: Domain to impersonate in TLS handshake (e.g. storage.googleapis.com)
MTProto.cfWorkerDomainHelp: Free per-user WebSocket relay (workers.dev). Recommended when media, reactions, or stickers fail to load. Reaches all data centers and avoids the shared-domain rate limits. Comma-separate multiple workers. Click the icon for setup.
MTProto.cfWorkerSetup: How to set up a Cloudflare Worker
MTProto.dcRelayHelp: External relay address (host:port) to reach Telegram DCs when blocked by IP
MTProto.dcRelayHelpButton: Show socat commands for relay setup
MTProto.dcRelayHelpIntro: Run these commands on your VPS to forward each Telegram DC to the relay port B4 uses. The DC list is fetched live from Telegram, so port mapping stays accurate even if Telegram adds new DCs.
MTProto.dcRelayHelpEmpty: Set the DC Relay address first (e.g. {{example}}). The base port determines the rest of the mapping.
MTProto.dcRelayHelpLoading: Fetching live DC list from Telegram…
MTProto.dcRelayHelpFailed: Could not fetch DC list. Try the Refresh button.
MTProto.dcRelayHelpHint: Open these ports on the VPS firewall: {{ports}}.
MTProto.relaySetup: DC Relay requires a VPS setup. Read the setup guide
MTProto.secretHelp: Paste this into Telegram MTProto proxy settings
MTProto.refreshDCsHint: Telegram DC list is cached locally. Refresh if your connection started failing.
MTProto.shareHostHelp: Address the recipient device will use to reach this proxy. LAN IP for local devices, public IP or DDNS hostname for remote use (port forwarding required).
MTProto.shareLinkHelp: Open this link on a device with Telegram installed to add the proxy.
MTProto.shareQrHelp: Scan with the phone camera to open in Telegram.
MTProto.upstreamDesc: How b4 reaches Telegram data centers. Used by the proxy server above and by the 'Telegram over WebSocket' routing mode in sets, so it applies even when the proxy server is off. Use WebSocket if your network blocks direct TCP to 149.154.x.x.
MTProto.upstreamBridgeNote: Transport mode applies to the proxy server only; the 'Telegram over WebSocket' routing mode always uses Auto (WebSocket-first with TCP fallback).
MTProto.upstreamTcpDesc: Fastest. Use when this host can reach 149.154.0.0/16 directly.
MTProto.upstreamAutoDesc: Try WebSocket first via kws*.web.telegram.org, fall back to direct TCP. Recommended for censored networks.
MTProto.upstreamWsDesc: Strict WebSocket transport via kws*.web.telegram.org. No TCP fallback.
MTProto.wsCustomDomainHelp: Optional extra domain (e.g. your-domain.com) that proxies WebSocket traffic to Telegram. b4 prepends kws1., kws2., ... per DC automatically. Independent of the CF proxy fallback below.
MTProto.wsEndpointHostHelp: Override the IP used when WS uses native kws*.web.telegram.org SNI. Default: 149.154.167.220. Leave empty for default. Does not affect the custom WebSocket domain path.
MTProto.cfProxyEnabledHelp: Use a rotating pool of Cloudflare-proxied domains as a fallback when Telegram's native edge can't reach a data center (notably DC 1, which is needed for media in foreign channels). The pool is refreshed once an hour from the URL below.
MTProto.cfProxyURLHelp: Source for the encoded CF-proxy domain list. Default is tg-ws-proxy's GitHub. Replace with your own if you host your own list.
MTProto.dcFallbackEnabledHelp: Telegram's data-center IP list is fetched from Telegram's official endpoint first. When that endpoint is blocked, b4 falls back to the mirror below. The mirror receives your IP address but no other data. Disable to use only Telegram's official endpoint.
MTProto.dcFallbackURLHelp: Source for the fallback data-center list, used only when Telegram's official endpoint is unreachable. Default is the b4 author's mirror. Set your own mirror here if you host one.
MTProto.testWsHelp: Probes DC 2 over the configured transport(s) and reports latency.
MTProto.testDirectTcpHelp: Probes DC 2 over direct TCP to Telegram, bypassing the DC Relay. Use to isolate whether issues come from the relay or from Telegram itself.
MTProto.upstreamAutoRelayDesc: DC Relay is configured: relay TCP is tried first, WebSocket as fallback.

MSSClamping.title: Global MSS Clamping
MSSClamping.description: Clamp TCP Maximum Segment Size to force data fragmentation
MSSClamping.enableDesc: Clamp MSS on all SYN packets via firewall rules (nftables/iptables)
MSSClamping.mssSizeHelp: Lower values = more fragmentation. 88 is commonly used for YouTube bypass.
MSSClamping.info: Reduces the TCP Maximum Segment Size on SYN/SYN-ACK packets for all TCP port 443 traffic, forcing data fragmentation. Most DPI systems cannot reassemble fragmented ClientHello. For per-device MSS clamping, use the Device Filtering settings.

Devices.title: Device Filtering
Devices.description: Filter traffic by source device MAC address
Devices.enableDesc: Only process traffic from selected devices
Devices.vendorLookupDesc: Download vendor database to identify device manufacturers (~6MB)
Devices.blacklistAlert: Blacklist mode: Selected devices will be EXCLUDED from DPI bypass
Devices.whitelistAlert: Whitelist mode: Only selected devices will use DPI bypass
Devices.arpUnavailable: ARP table not available. Device discovery unavailable.
Devices.manualDevicesDesc: Add IP addresses of devices behind another router that are not visible in the ARP table. These devices will appear in device lists and can be used for per-device filtering.

RestartDialog.title: Restart B4 Service
RestartDialog.subtitle: System Service Management
RestartDialog.warning: This will restart the B4 service. The web interface will be temporarily unavailable during the restart.
RestartDialog.restarting: Service is restarting, waiting for reconnection...
RestartDialog.timeout: Service restart timed out. Please check manually.
RestartDialog.doNotClose: Please wait, do not close this window...

Api.alert: Here you can setup API settings for different services that can be used by B4.
Api.ipinfoDescription: Configure your IPINFO.IO API token here.

Ai.title: AI Assistant
Ai.description: Bring-your-own-key AI assistance for explaining settings and troubleshooting.
Ai.privacyAlert: When using a cloud provider (OpenAI, Anthropic), parts of your config and questions you ask are sent to that provider. For full privacy, run a local model with Ollama.
Ai.enableDesc: Show AI explain/help controls in the UI
Ai.providerHelp: Choose a cloud provider or a local Ollama instance
Ai.modelHelp: Pick from the provider's list, or type a custom model id
Ai.endpointHelp: Override the API base URL (leave default for the official provider)
Ai.keyDialogHelp: The key is stored on the server in a separate file (mode 0600), not in the main config. Reference: {{ref}}

Checker.title: Testing Configuration
Checker.discoveryTimeoutHelp: Max time to wait for each connection attempt when testing presets during discovery. Increase if you have a slow or unstable connection.
Checker.configPropagationHelp: Wait time after applying each preset before testing it. Allows the new configuration to reach all workers. Increase on slow or resource-constrained devices where config updates take longer to settle.
Checker.referenceDomainHelp: A known accessible domain used to measure your raw network speed without DPI bypass. Discovery compares preset results against this baseline to evaluate performance. Choose a fast, reliable domain that is not blocked in your region.
Checker.addDnsHelp: DNS servers used during discovery to detect DNS poisoning and find a working bypass. Each server is tested with plain and fragmented queries to determine if it can resolve blocked domains correctly.
Checker.activeDns: Active DNS servers for poisoning detection:

Watchdog.title: Watchdog
Watchdog.intervalHelp: How often to check each domain when healthy.
Watchdog.failureIntervalHelp: How often to re-check a domain after a failure is detected.
Watchdog.cooldownHelp: Minimum time between discovery runs for the same domain.
Watchdog.timeoutHelp: Max time to wait for each health check request.
Watchdog.maxRetriesHelp: Consecutive failures before triggering automatic discovery.
Watchdog.addDomainHelp: Domains to monitor for DPI blocking.

Geo.alert: GeoSite and GeoIP database files for domain and IP categorization.
Geo.alertSub: Each database can be downloaded independently from different sources.
Geo.title: Geo Databases
Geo.destDirHelp: Directory where .dat files will be saved
Geo.selectSource: Please select a source or enter a URL
Geo.autoUpdateOnStartupDesc: Re-download both files when b4 starts. Missing files (e.g. when stored in /tmp) are always re-downloaded if a source URL is set.
Geo.autoUpdateIntervalHelp: Run periodic refresh in the background using the configured source URLs

Backup.alert: Create a backup of your B4 configuration directory or restore from a previous backup. Backups include configuration, discovery history, detector history, device aliases, and captured payloads. Geodat files (.dat) and OUI database are excluded.
Backup.downloadDescription: Generate and download a tar.gz archive of your B4 configuration
Backup.downloadInfo: The backup will include all files from the configuration directory except geodat (.dat) files and the OUI database.
Backup.restoreDescription: Upload a previously downloaded backup to restore your configuration
Backup.restoreInfo: Upload a .tar.gz backup file. Files will be extracted into the configuration directory. A service restart is recommended after restoring.
Backup.restoreSuccess: Backup restored successfully. Restart B4 to apply changes.

Capture.alert: Generate optimized TLS ClientHello with SNI-first extension order
Capture.alertSub: Instant generation for DPI bypass. One payload per domain. Use in Faking → Captured Payload
Capture.uploadDescription: Upload your own binary payload file (max 64KB)
Capture.nameDomainHelp: Name associated with the uploaded payload
Capture.protocolHelp: Auto-detected from file contents on upload. Override only if needed.
Capture.domainHelp: Enter domain to generate payload for
Capture.generateSuccess: Generated optimized payload for {{domain}} (SNI-first for DPI bypass)
Capture.generatedDesc: {{count}} optimized payload(s) ready for use (SNI-first for DPI bypass)
Capture.emptyDesc: Enter a domain above and click Generate to create an optimized payload
Capture.hexSubtitle: Copy for use in Faking → Custom Payload

health.resetConfirm: Reset all statistics? This will clear all connection counters, device activity, and domain statistics. The service will continue running.

manager.title: Configuration Sets
manager.description: Manage bypass configurations for different domains and scenarios
manager.noSetsHint: Create your first set to start configuring bypass rules

deleteDialog.title: Delete Configuration Set
deleteDialog.subtitle: This action cannot be undone
deleteDialog.confirm: Are you sure you want to delete

batchDeleteDialog.title: Delete Configuration Sets
batchDeleteDialog.subtitle: This action cannot be undone
batchDeleteDialog.confirm: Are you sure you want to delete the following sets?

targets.sectionDescription: Configure domain matching for DPI bypass and blocking
targets.escalateHelper: If this set keeps failing for a site, b4 will use the chosen set for that site instead. After a while, b4 tries this set again.
targets.domainAlert: Domains in this list will use DPI bypass techniques (fragmentation, faking) when matched.
targets.manualDomainsTooltip: Add specific domains to bypass DPI.
targets.geositeCatTooltip: Load predefined domain lists from GeoSite database for DPI bypass
targets.ipAlert: IP ranges in these categories will use DPI bypass techniques (fragmentation, faking) when matched.
targets.manualIpsTooltip: Add specific ip/cidr to bypass DPI.
targets.geoipCatTooltip: Load predefined IP lists from GeoIP database for DPI bypass
targets.deviceAlert: Restrict this set to specific source devices. When devices are selected, this set will only apply to traffic from those devices. Device-specific sets take priority over general sets.
targets.arpUnavailable: ARP table not available. Device discovery is unavailable. Source device filtering requires a working ARP table.

tcp.sectionDescription: Configure TCP packet handling and DPI bypass techniques

general.seg2delayHelper: Delay between TCP segments. Use a range for random delay per packet.
general.portFilterHelper: Match specific TCP ports (Telegram, XMPP, etc.) - port 443 is always included
general.dropSackDesc: Strip Selective Acknowledgment from TCP headers to confuse stateful DPI
general.dupAlert: Some ISPs throttle by randomly dropping outgoing packets to specific IP ranges (e.g. Telegram subnets). Duplication sends multiple copies of each packet. When enabled, all other DPI evasion is bypassed for this set. Applies to all configured TCP ports.
general.dupEnableDesc: Drop original packet and send multiple copies via raw socket
general.dupCountHelper: Number of packet copies to send (original is dropped)
general.ibdAlert: Some IPs are blocked entirely by the firewall (not by domain name). When this happens, all bypass strategies fail and the connection hangs for 30+ seconds. This feature detects such blocks and immediately resets the connection, so your device can retry on a different server.
general.ibdEnableDesc: Detect IP-level blocks and send RST to force fast retry
general.ibdThresholdHelper: How many unanswered retransmissions before declaring the IP blocked
general.ibdThresholdWarn: A low threshold may cause false positives — a single dropped packet or slow server could be mistaken for an IP block.
general.ibdTimeoutHelper: Alternative trigger: time since first attempt with no response
general.ibdCacheDesc: Remember blocked IPs so future connections get instant reset without waiting
general.rstAlert: DPI systems can inject fake TCP RST packets to kill your connections. This feature records the TTL (hop count) from the real server's first response and drops any RST packet whose TTL doesn't match — a strong indicator that it was injected from a different network position.
general.rstEnableDesc: Drop RST packets with mismatched TTL to prevent DPI-injected connection resets
general.rstTtlToleranceHelper: Maximum allowed difference between the server's reference TTL and an incoming RST packet's TTL
general.mssEnableDesc: Override TCP MSS for connections matching this set's IP, GeoIP, or device targets
general.mssScopeHint: MSS is set on the TCP SYN, before the TLS handshake. It can only be scoped by IP / GeoIP / source device. SNI domain, GeoSite and TLS version filters do not participate in MSS clamping.
general.mssScopeRequired: Add an IP, GeoIP, or source device target before enabling per-set MSS.
general.mssSizeHelper: Typical TSPU workaround for Smart TVs on YouTube: 88

splitting.strategyPoolDesc: Select multiple strategies to randomize per connection. When active, a random strategy is picked from the pool for each new packet. Leave empty to use the selected method above.
splitting.strategyPoolActive: {{count}} strategies in pool — each connection will use a random one
splitting.oobAlert: Inserts a byte with TCP URG flag. Server ignores it, but stateful DPI gets confused.
splitting.tlsRecAlert: Splits ClientHello into multiple TLS records. DPI expecting single-record handshake fails to match.
splitting.tlsRecPositionHelper: First TLS record size in bytes
splitting.disabledWarning: Fragmentation disabled. Only fake packets (if enabled) will be used for bypass.

combo.alert: Combo combines multiple split points and sends segments out of order with timing jitter to confuse stateful DPI.
combo.decoyDesc: Send fake ClientHello with whitelisted SNI before real traffic
combo.sniSplitDesc: Split in middle of SNI hostname
combo.noSplits: No splits enabled - packet sent as single segment
combo.shuffleHelper: How to reorder segments before sending
combo.shuffleMiddleDesc: First and last segments stay in place, only the segments between them are shuffled
combo.shuffleFullDesc: All segments, including first and last, are randomly reordered before sending
combo.shuffleReverseDesc: Segments are sent in completely reversed order — last segment first, first segment last
combo.jitterMaxHelper: Max random delay between other segments (μs)
combo.fakePerSegAlert: sends fake overlap packets before each real segment, not just the first. Floods DPI reassembly with garbage.
combo.fakePerSegDesc: Send fake overlap before every segment
combo.fakesPerSegHelper: Number of fake packets before each segment
combo.noSplitPointsWarning: No split points enabled. Enable at least one for Combo to work effectively.

disorder.alert: Disorder sends real TCP segments out of order with timing jitter. No fake packets — exploits DPI that expects sequential data.
disorder.sniSplitDesc: Split around SNI hostname for targeted disruption
disorder.segRandomOrder: Segments sent in random order (example shown)
disorder.timingAlert: Random delay between segments. Used when TCP Seg2Delay is 0.
disorder.jitterWarning: Max jitter should be greater than min jitter for random variation.
disorder.fakePerSegAlert: Multidisorder: sends fake overlap packets before each real segment, not just the first. Floods DPI reassembly with garbage.
disorder.fakePerSegDesc: Send fake overlap before every segment
disorder.fakesPerSegHelper: Number of fake packets before each segment
disorder.seqOverlapAlert: Prepends fake bytes with decreased TCP sequence number. DPI sees fake protocol header, server discards overlap.
disorder.seqOverlapLengthHelper: How many pattern bytes to prepend to a real segment, with its sequence number shifted back by the same amount (0 = off).
disorder.seqovlNote: DPI sees fake header at decreased seq#, server reassembles correctly
disorder.addByteEmpty: Add hex bytes for custom pattern

extSplit.alert: Automatically splits TLS ClientHello just before the SNI extension. DPI sees incomplete extension list and fails to parse SNI.
extSplit.splitNote: Split happens at the yellow line — before SNI extension starts
extSplit.noConfigNeeded: No configuration needed. Uses Reverse Order toggle above and Seg2 Delay from TCP tab.

firstByte.alert: Sends just 1 byte, waits, then sends the rest. Exploits DPI timeout — incomplete TLS record can't be parsed.
firstByte.vizNote: DPI sees 1 byte (TLS record type), waits for more, times out before SNI arrives
firstByte.noConfigNeeded: No configuration needed. Delay controlled by Seg2 Delay in TCP tab (minimum 100ms applied automatically).

tcpIp.smartSniSplitDesc: Automatically split in the middle of the SNI hostname (recommended)
tcpIp.splitMode3seg: 3 segments: split at fixed position AND middle of SNI
tcpIp.splitModeSni: 2 segments: split at middle of SNI hostname
tcpIp.splitModeFixed: 2 segments: split at byte {{pos}} of TLS payload
tcpIp.manualOverride: Manual override — use if Smart SNI Split doesn't work for your ISP
tcpIp.fixedSplitPosHelper: Bytes from TLS payload start (0 = disabled)
tcpIp.bothEnabled: Both enabled → packet splits into 3 segments

fakeSni.title: Fake SNI Packets
fakeSni.enableDesc: Send fake SNI packets before real ClientHello
fakeSni.strategyHelper: How to make fake packets unprocessable by server
fakeSni.payloadDomainHelper: TLS ClientHello will be generated with this domain as SNI
fakeSni.customPayloadHelper: Hex-encoded payload for fake packets (use Capture feature to get real payloads)
fakeSni.noPayloads: No TLS payloads available. Generate optimized payloads (SNI-first for DPI bypass) or upload your own.
fakeSni.noPayloadsHelper: No payloads available. Generate one in Settings first.
fakeSni.navigateSettings: Navigate to Settings to generate or upload TLS ClientHello payloads.
fakeSni.ttlHelper: TTL for fake packets (should expire before server)
fakeSni.seqOffsetHelper: TCP sequence number offset for pastseq strategy
fakeSni.timestampDecreaseHelper: Amount to decrease TCP timestamp option (default: 600000)
fakeSni.packetCountHelper: Number of fake packets to send
fakeSni.tlsModDesc: Modify fake TLS ClientHello to improve bypass
fakeSni.randomizeTlsRandomDesc: Replace 32-byte Random field in fake packets
fakeSni.dupSessionIdDesc: Copy Session ID from real ClientHello into fake

synFake.title: SYN Fake Packets
synFake.alert: Send fake SYN packets during the TCP handshake phase to pre-confuse DPI state tracking before the real connection starts.
synFake.enableDesc: Send fake SYN packets during handshake (aggressive technique)
synFake.md5Desc: Send fake SYN with TCP MD5 option before real handshake
synFake.payloadLenHelper: 0 = header only, >0 = add fake TLS payload
synFake.ttlHelper: TTL value for SYN fake packets (default 3 if unset)

desync.title: TCP Desync Attack
desync.alert: Desync attacks inject fake TCP control packets (RST/FIN/ACK) with corrupted checksums and low TTL. These packets confuse stateful DPI systems but are discarded by the real server.
desync.descRst: Inject fake RST packets with bad checksums to disrupt DPI state tracking
desync.descFin: Inject fake FIN packets with past sequence numbers to confuse connection state
desync.descAck: Inject fake ACK packets with random future sequence/ack numbers
desync.descCombo: Send RST + FIN + ACK sequence for stronger desync effect
desync.descFull: Full attack: fake SYN, overlapping RSTs, PSH, and URG packets
desync.ttlHelperEnabled: Low TTL ensures packets expire before reaching server
desync.countHelperEnabled: Number of fake packets per desync attack
desync.postDesyncDesc: Send fake RST after ClientHello to evict connection from DPI tracking table

window.title: Window Manipulation
window.alert: Window manipulation sends fake ACK packets with modified TCP window sizes before your real packet. These fakes use low TTL so they expire before reaching the server but confuse middlebox DPI.
window.descOff: No window manipulation - use actual TCP window
window.descZero: Send fake packets: first with window=0, then window=65535
window.descRandom: Send 3-5 fake packets with random window sizes from your list
window.descOscillate: Cycle through your custom window values sequentially
window.oscillateHint: Packets will cycle through these values in order
window.randomHint: Random values will be picked from this list
window.emptyValues: No values configured - defaults will be used

incoming.title: Incoming Response Bypass
incoming.alert: Manipulates incoming server responses to bypass DPI that throttles connections after receiving ~15-20KB. Injects fake packets toward server that DPI sees but die before reaching destination.
incoming.descFake: Inject corrupted ACK packets toward server with low TTL on every incoming data packet
incoming.descReset: Inject fake RST packets when incoming bytes threshold reached
incoming.descFin: Inject fake FIN packets when incoming bytes threshold reached
incoming.descDesync: Inject RST+FIN+ACK combo when incoming bytes threshold reached
incoming.stratDescBadsum: Corrupt TCP checksum only - packets dropped by kernel
incoming.stratDescBadseq: Corrupt sequence number - packets ignored by TCP stack
incoming.stratDescBadack: Corrupt ACK number - packets ignored by TCP stack
incoming.stratDescRand: Randomly pick corruption method per packet
incoming.stratDescAll: Apply all corruptions: bad seq + bad ack + bad checksum
incoming.ttlHelper: Low TTL ensures fakes expire before reaching server
incoming.fakeCountHelper: Number of fake packets per injection
incoming.thresholdMinFake: Not used in fake mode (triggers on every packet)
incoming.thresholdMaxHelper: Threshold randomized between min-max per connection

mutation.title: ClientHello Mutation
mutation.descRandom: Randomize extension order and add noise
mutation.descGrease: Insert GREASE extensions to confuse DPI
mutation.descPadding: Add padding extension to reach target size
mutation.greaseCountHelper: Number of GREASE extensions to insert
mutation.fakeExtCountHelper: Number of fake TLS extensions to insert
mutation.fakeSniHelper: Additional SNI values to inject into ClientHello

udp.sectionDescription: Configure UDP packet processing and QUIC filtering
udp.quicAllDesc: Match all QUIC Initial packets (blind matching)
udp.quicParseDesc: Match only QUIC with SNI in domain list (smart matching)
udp.portFilterHelper: Match specific UDP ports (VoIP, gaming, etc.) - leave empty to disable
udp.filterStunDesc: Ignore STUN packets (recommended for voice/video calls)
udp.parseWarning: Parse mode requires domains: Add domains in the Targets section for SNI matching to work. Without domains, no QUIC traffic will be processed.
udp.noProcessingWarning: UDP processing disabled: Enable QUIC filtering or add port filters to process UDP traffic. Currently, all UDP packets will pass through unchanged.
udp.modeDropDesc: Drop matched UDP packets (forces TCP fallback)
udp.modeRejectDesc: Send ICMP Port Unreachable back to client (fastest TCP fallback)
udp.modeFakeDesc: Send fake packets and fragment real ones (DPI bypass)
udp.fakeModeInfo: Fake mode: Matched UDP packets will be preceded by fake packets and fragmented to bypass DPI systems. Configure fake packet settings below.
udp.rejectModeInfo: Reject mode: Matched UDP packets will be dropped and an ICMP Port Unreachable response will be sent back to the client, causing immediate TCP fallback without waiting for timeouts.
udp.dropModeInfo: Drop mode: Matched UDP packets will be dropped, forcing the application to fall back to TCP (e.g., QUIC → HTTPS).
udp.strategyTtlDesc: Low TTL so fakes expire before reaching the server
udp.strategyChecksumDesc: Invalid UDP checksum so the server drops fakes
udp.fakeCountHelper: Number of fake packets sent before real packet
udp.fakeSizeHelper: Size of each fake UDP packet payload
udp.fakePayloadFileHelper: Pick a captured payload to use as the fake UDP packet body. Truncated or zero-padded to the size above. Default = zero fill.
udp.fakePayloadFileEmpty: No captures uploaded yet. Default = zero fill.
udp.fakePayloadFileAutoQuic: b4 generates a fresh randomized QUIC Initial for each fake packet, with random connection IDs. Recommended size: 1200 bytes.
udp.fakePayloadManage: Upload or manage payloads in Settings → Payloads
udp.seg2delayHelper: Delay between segments. Use a range for random delay per packet.

dns.sectionDescription: Redirect DNS queries to bypass ISP DNS poisoning
dns.alert: Some ISPs intercept DNS queries (especially to 8.8.8.8) and return fake IPs for blocked domains. DNS redirect transparently rewrites your DNS queries to use an unpoisoned resolver.
dns.enableDesc: Redirect DNS queries for domains in this set to specified DNS server
dns.fragmentQueryDesc: Split DNS packets using IP fragmentation to bypass DPI that pattern-matches domain names in queries
dns.serverIpHelper: Select below or enter custom IP
dns.noServerWarning: Select or enter a DNS server IP to enable redirect.
dns.googleWarning: Google DNS (8.8.8.8) is commonly poisoned by Russian ISPs. Consider Quad9 (9.9.9.9) or Cloudflare (1.1.1.1) instead.

routing.sectionDescription: DNS redirect and traffic routing for matched domains
routing.enableDesc: Resolve matched domains and route destination IPs via selected output interface or upstream proxy
routing.modeHelper: Send matched traffic out a network interface, or hand it off to an upstream SOCKS5 proxy (e.g. local Xray/sing-box)
routing.flowMTProtoWSCaption: Matched Telegram traffic is bridged over Telegram's WebSocket edge by B4 - no in-app proxy and no VPS needed.
routing.howItWorksMTProtoWS: When a device connects to a Telegram datacenter, B4 transparently intercepts it and relays the session over Telegram's WebSocket edge (with Cloudflare fallback), so Telegram works without configuring a proxy inside the app. Pair this with the 'telegram' geoip target. Uses the shared 'Telegram upstream' settings (Settings -> MTProto), which apply even when the proxy server is off. Best-effort: only TCP MTProto is bridged (voice calls and unknown transports fall through directly).
routing.infoMTProtoWS: Routing intercepts matched Telegram traffic from the source interfaces above and bridges it over the WebSocket edge. Connections B4 cannot map to a datacenter fall open to a direct connection.
routing.mtprotoWsNote: This mode runs on its own - the MTProto proxy under Settings -> MTProto does not need to be enabled. Matched Telegram traffic is relayed over B4's built-in WebSocket endpoint, with a Cloudflare fallback.
routing.blockActionHelper: How matched traffic is blocked. Reject fails the connection immediately (TCP reset, or ICMP unreachable for UDP/QUIC); Drop is silent.
routing.flowBlockCaption: Matched destinations are dropped or rejected by B4 - traffic never leaves the router.
routing.howItWorksBlock: When a device on your network connects to a matched domain or IP, B4 blocks it outright instead of routing it anywhere. Add ad/tracker domains, IPs, or geo categories to this set's targets. 'Reject' (the default) fails the connection instantly (TCP reset, or ICMP unreachable for UDP/QUIC); 'Drop' silently blackholes it so the connection just times out. Works for both LAN devices (forward) and the router itself (output).
routing.infoBlock: Routing blocks this set's matched domains and IPs. No output interface or upstream is needed.
routing.outputInterfaceHelper: Select egress interface for this set
routing.upstreamHostHelper: Hostname or IP of the SOCKS5 proxy. Use 127.0.0.1 if Xray/sing-box runs on the same router.
routing.upstreamAuthHelper: Leave empty if the upstream proxy does not require authentication
routing.useDomainDesc: When B4 already knows the domain for the destination IP (from prior DNS), pass the domain to the upstream so it can resolve and pick a geo-correct IP. Recommended.
routing.failOpen: Fall back to direct on upstream failure
routing.failOpenDesc: If the upstream proxy is unreachable, open a plain direct connection to the original destination instead of failing.
routing.failOpenWarning: Fail-open exposes the destination to your ISP if the upstream goes down. Disable for whitelist-mode regions where direct connections do not work and you want failures to be visible.
routing.udpDesc: Also tunnel matched UDP traffic through the upstream SOCKS5 proxy (via UDP ASSOCIATE), not just TCP. Applies to forwarded traffic from LAN clients. Leave off for TCP-only routing.
routing.proxyManipulationNote: In proxy mode, packet manipulation (faking, fragmentation, desync) is disabled for matched traffic. The upstream proxy handles obfuscation.
routing.flowProxyCaption: Matched traffic is handed off to the upstream SOCKS5 proxy. The proxy is responsible for reaching the destination.
routing.howItWorksProxy: When a device on your network visits a matched domain, B4 intercepts the connection and forwards it through the upstream SOCKS5 proxy you configure. Use this to chain B4 into Xray/sing-box running locally on the router or on another host. Useful in regions with whitelist-based blocking where direct connections to non-whitelisted IPs are dropped.
routing.ipTtlHelper: How long resolved A/AAAA addresses stay in routing set
routing.sourceInterfacesHint: Limit marking to ingress interfaces (empty = any interface)
routing.unavailableWarning: Output interface "{{iface}}" is down or missing. Routing for this set will not work until it is available again or you select another interface.
routing.info: Routing uses DNS answers for this set domains. Route marks and table IDs are assigned automatically per output interface.
routing.infoProxy: Routing uses DNS answers for this set domains. Matched traffic is hijacked from the source interfaces above and forwarded to the upstream proxy.
routing.flowCaption: DNS queries for this set's domains are intercepted. Resolved IPs are routed through the selected output interface.
routing.howItWorks: When a device on your network visits a matched domain, B4 intercepts the DNS response, learns the destination IP, and adds a routing rule so that traffic to that IP goes through the output interface you choose - for example, a VPN or a different WAN link.
routing.sourceInterfacesDesc: Choose which network interfaces B4 should watch for traffic. For example, select your LAN or Wi-Fi bridge to only route traffic from devices on that network. Leave empty to route traffic from all interfaces.
routing.ipTtlDesc: After B4 learns an IP address from a DNS response, it keeps the routing rule for this many seconds. If the domain's IP changes (e.g. CDN rotation), the old rule expires and B4 picks up the new IP on the next DNS query.

importExport.importSuccess: Configuration imported successfully! Review your changes in the other tabs, then click Save to apply.
importExport.infoAlert: Copy JSON to share this set, or paste a configuration to import it.
importExport.jsonHelper: Paste or type a set configuration JSON to import it.
importExport.deviceFilterWarning: This set has source device filters configured. They are excluded from export as they are device-specific.
importExport.invalidFields: Invalid set configuration: missing required fields

editor.escalateNeedsRstProtection: Tip: also enable RST Injection Protection in the TCP tab. It helps keep the current page working while b4 detects the block.

escalation.sectionDescription: If this set keeps failing to open a website, b4 will use another set for that website next time.
escalation.rstThresholdHelper: How many blocked connections to the same site before b4 switches to the backup set.
escalation.rstWindowHelper: Failures only count if they happen within this time. Older ones are forgotten.
escalation.ttlHelper: After switching, the site stays on the backup set for this long. Then b4 tries this set again.

discovery.title: Configuration Discovery
discovery.description: Hierarchical testing: Strategy Detection → Optimization → Combination
discovery.alert: Configuration Discovery: Automatically test multiple configuration presets to find the most effective DPI bypass settings for the domains you specify below. B4 will temporarily apply different configurations and measure their performance.
discovery.addDomainHelper: Press Enter to add. Paste multiple URLs separated by commas or newlines.
discovery.addDomainToSetFailed: Failed to add domain to set
discovery.foundWorking: Found a working configuration! Still testing {{remaining}} more configs — a faster option may be found.
discovery.transportBlocked: Transport blocked: This domain is blocked at the IP/network level — TCP connections cannot be established to any known IP address. DPI bypass strategies cannot help with IP-level blocking. A VPN or proxy is required for this domain.
discovery.allFailed: All {{count}} tested configurations failed for this domain.

history.transportBlockedShort: Transport blocked — IP-level blocking detected. A VPN or proxy is required.

options.title: Discovery Options
options.cacheHint: Previously successful configurations are tested first.
options.tlsVersionHint: TLS version used for discovery probes. Some DPI systems handle TLS 1.2 and 1.3 differently.
options.customPayloadsHint: Test with generated TLS ClientHello (SNI-first) instead of built-in payloads
options.validationTriesHint: Number of successful connection attempts required to validate a preset
options.noPayloadsSuffix: to test with custom TLS ClientHello.

detector.title: TSPU / DPI Detector
detector.description: Detect ISP-level Deep Packet Inspection and blocking
detector.alert: DPI Detector: Runs diagnostic tests to detect TSPU (Technical System for Countering Threats) and ISP-level internet blocking. Tests DNS integrity, domain accessibility via TLS/HTTP, and TCP connection drops at characteristic byte thresholds.

descriptions.dns: Compares UDP DNS vs DoH to detect spoofing and interception
descriptions.domains: Probes blocked domains via TLS 1.3, TLS 1.2, and HTTP
descriptions.tcp: Detects TSPU connection drops via keep-alive requests with increasing header size
descriptions.sni: Finds bypass SNI values for blocked ASNs by brute-forcing whitelist domains
descriptions.dns-availability: Measures per-provider DNS latency over UDP and DoH (RFC 8484)
descriptions.telegram: Measures Telegram download/upload throughput and datacenter reachability

alerts.dohBlocked: All DNS-over-HTTPS servers are blocked. Your ISP is filtering encrypted DNS.
alerts.udpBlocked: All UDP DNS servers (port 53) are blocked.
alerts.stubIps: Stub/sinkhole IPs detected: {{ips}}. Multiple blocked domains resolve to these IPs.

legend.title: Legend — how to read the results

meanings.DNS_SPOOFING: UDP DNS returns a different IP than DoH (spoofing)
meanings.DNS_INTERCEPTION: UDP DNS is intercepted or times out while DoH works
meanings.FAKE_NXDOMAIN: UDP returns NXDOMAIN while DoH resolves the domain
meanings.FAKE_EMPTY: UDP returns an empty answer while DoH resolves the domain
meanings.BOTH_UNAVAILABLE: Neither UDP nor DoH resolution is available
meanings.TLS_SPOOF: Non-TLS or garbage response injected in place of TLS
meanings.TLS_ALERT: Forged TLS alert (e.g. unrecognized name / handshake failure)
meanings.TLS_RST: Connection reset during or after the TLS handshake
meanings.SYN_DROP: TCP SYN dropped, connection never established
meanings.TCP16: Connection dropped within the 12-69KB TSPU fat-flow window
meanings.ISP_PAGE: Redirected to an ISP block page
meanings.FOUND: A working bypass SNI was found
meanings.NOT_BLOCKED: ASN was not blocked, no brute-force needed

controlBar.filterPlaceholder: Filter (combine with +, exclude with !, e.g. tcp+!domain:google.com)

table.enrichNoAsn: No ASN found for this IP

addDomain.title: Add Domain to Manual List
addDomain.alert: Select which domain pattern to add to the manual domains list. More specific patterns will only match exact subdomains, while broader patterns will match all subdomains.

addIp.title: Add IP/CIDR to Manual List
addIp.alert: Select the desired IP or CIDR range. You can enrich with network information to load all ASN prefixes.

update.title: Version Management
update.dockerDesc: To update, pull the latest image and recreate your container:
update.inProgress: Update in progress. Waiting for service to restart...
update.manualCheck: Update may have completed but service did not restart. Please check manually.
update.backupWarning: Back up your configuration before updating. Use Settings → Backup to download a backup.

watchdog.title: Watchdog Monitor
watchdog.description: Domain health monitoring and automatic healing
watchdog.disabledHint: Watchdog is disabled. Enable it in <link>Settings > Discovery</link> to start monitoring domains.
watchdog.noDomainsHint: No domains configured. Add domains in <link>Settings > Discovery</link> > Watchdog section.
watchdog.addPlaceholder: Add domain to monitor, e.g. youtube.com

errors.port_in_use_system: Port {{port}} on {{bind}} is already in use
errors.out_of_range: Value is out of allowed range
errors.tls_pair_required: Both TLS certificate and key must be specified together
errors.geosite_path_missing: Geosite path must be configured to use geosite categories
errors.geoip_path_missing: Geoip path must be configured to use geoip categories
errors.socks5_loop: Set "{{set}}": upstream proxy points to b4's own SOCKS5 server (loop)
errors.mark_conflict: Mark value {{mark}} conflicts with reserved bits
